title: Red Sox get discouraging update on Roman Anthony’s injury after he tries swinging bat
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony experienced finger soreness swinging a bat, delaying his return from a sprained right ring finger.
Red Sox interim manager Chad Tracy announced Anthony would rest until Thursday, missing some baseball activities.
Anthony initially injured his finger on May 4 and had hoped to return by May 15 but now eyes a later recovery.

title: Bill Belichick gets candid on Tom Brady’s growth with Patriots: ‘He wasn’t even really good, but he became great’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bill Belichick discussed Tom Brady's progression from a fourth-string quarterback to NFL greatness on 'Pardon My Take'.
Despite being initially underestimated, Tom Brady's dedication and growth helped him become a central figure in Patriots' successes.
Belichick, now coaching at UNC, reflected on the differences and challenges of coaching college football versus the NFL.

title: As Minogue pours millions into his own campaign, state lawmaker pushes to ban self-funding
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: GOP candidate Mike Minogue contributed $13.5 million to his campaign for governor of Massachusetts.
State Senator Rebecca Rausch filed an amendment to ban unlimited self-funding of political campaigns.
Minogue's wealth significantly exceeds his opponents, with over $10 million in cash on hand for his campaign.

title: Celtics fans voted NBA’s ‘most obnoxious’ in anonymous player poll
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics fans were voted NBA's 'most passionate' and 'most obnoxious' in an anonymous player poll by The Athletic.
TD Garden fans received 32% of the vote for 'most obnoxious', surpassing Philadelphia's Xfinity Mobile arena (17.9%).
Boston was not in the top eight destinations for Giannis Antetokounmpo, with Milwaukee leading at 45.3% in player opinions.

title: Stephen A. Smith responds to Jaylen Brown’s comments: ‘Be careful what you wish for’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticized Stephen A. Smith on Twitch, calling him the 'face of clickbait media' and suggesting he retire.
Stephen A. Smith responded on 'First Take,' advising Brown to consider potential negative media attention before speaking out.
Celtics lost Game 7 to the 76ers, with Brown scoring 33 points as Tatum was out due to knee stiffness.

title: FAA awarding Logan Airport $2.8 million for family-friendly upgrades
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Logan Airport receives $2.8 million from FAA for upgrading four Kidport play areas in Terminals A, B, and C.
FAA is distributing $970 million to U.S. airports to enhance family-friendly facilities through the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.
Other grants include $2 million to Tupelo Airport for security lanes and $10 million to Palm Beach for family-oriented rooms.

title: Beverly mansion robbery suspect indicted on 10 charges
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Emajae Brown indicted for Beverly mansion robbery, facing 10 charges including armed burglary and kidnapping.
Brown allegedly stole nearly $8 million in valuables and assaulted a housekeeper at 'Rock Edge' mansion.
Police identified Brown using surveillance technology; $300,000 in cash and gold found at his arrest.

title: Boston police commissioner questions previous sentence for alleged Cambridge shooter
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston Police Commissioner Michael Cox criticized the lenient sentence given to alleged Cambridge shooter Tyler Brown for a 2020 offense.
Tyler Brown received a five to six-year sentence for a 2020 shooting, despite prosecutors seeking a 10 to 12-year term.
Cox expressed concern about Brown's release, linking it to his alleged involvement in a recent Cambridge shooting incident.

title: Should Boston host the 2028 Democratic National Convention?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston is a finalist among five cities for the 2028 Democratic National Convention, alongside Atlanta, Chicago, Denver, and Philadelphia.
State and city officials, including Gov. Maura Healey, will showcase potential convention venues during DNC leaders' tour of Boston.
Hosting the convention could generate up to $400 million in economic benefits for Boston, according to Boston 2028 Chair Steve Kerrigan.

title: Early thoughts on the Celtics’ offseason: Red flags, silver linings, and biggest needs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics eliminated in playoffs after leading 3-1 series, marking first such loss in 80-year history.
Player development highlighted as a strength with key contributions from Neemias Queta, Baylor Scheierman, and others.
Key offseason needs include an offensive-minded big man and a veteran two-guard to boost bench scoring.
